meta.name: Process vendor invoices with UploadToURL, AWS Textract, and Google Sheets

parameters.content:
## invoice data with UploadToURL, AWS Textract, and Google Sheets
The Problem: Manually keying data from paper or PDF invoices into spreadsheets is slow and prone to human error.
The Solution: An automated Accounts Payable pipeline that hosts scans via UploadToURL, extracts data via AWS Textract, and logs it to Google Sheets.

⚙️ How it Works
Webhook: Receives an invoice scan (Binary or URL).

UploadToURL: Hosts the file and returns a clean CDN link for the audit trail.

AWS Textract: Automatically reads the document to extract Vendor, Amount, and Due Date.

Data Sync: Checks for duplicates and logs the extracted info to Google Sheets and Slack.

🔐 Credentials & Setup
Node: Install n8n-nodes-uploadtourl via Community Nodes.

APIs: UploadToURL, AWS (Textract), Google Sheets, and Slack.

Variables: Set GSHEET_SPREADSHEET_ID and SLACK_FINANCE_CHANNEL.

## 1 — Intake & upload

**Webhook → Validate → Has Remote URL? → Upload to URL (×2) → Extract CDN URL**

Accepts `POST` with `fileUrl` or binary PDF/image. Validates file extension against allowlist (`pdf`, `jpg`, `jpeg`, `png`, `tiff`). UploadToURL hosts the file via the native community node. Both upload paths normalise into a single CDN URL.

## 2 — OCR extraction

**AWS Textract → Parse & Validate Extracted Data**

Textract runs `AnalyzeExpense` on the CDN URL and returns structured expense fields. The parse node extracts: vendor name, invoice number, total amount, currency, and due date. Flags any missing required fields as `incomplete` status rather than failing hard — so partial extractions still get recorded.

## 3 — Duplicate check & Sheets write

**Search Sheets → IF Duplicate? → Append Row or Update Row**

Searches the Google Sheet for an existing row matching the invoice number before writing. Duplicates get flagged with status `duplicate` and skipped. New invoices are appended with status `pending`. Existing rows with the same number but `incomplete` status get updated with the new extraction.

## 4 — Notification & response

**Slack → Build Response → Respond to Webhook**

Slack posts to the finance channel with vendor, amount, due date, status badge, and a direct link to the CDN file. Webhook returns `201` with the full invoice record including the Google Sheets row ID.

nodes.name: AWS Textract - Analyse Expense
nodes.notes: Calls Textract AnalyzeExpense. Alternatively set TEXTRACT_S3_BUCKET variable and upload the CDN file to S3 first, or use the AWS SDK via a Code node if you prefer direct API calls with SigV4 signing.

nodes.name: Parse & Validate Extracted Data
nodes.notes: Extracts vendor, invoice number, amount, due date, currency, issue date, and line items from Textract response. Partial extractions are allowed — missing fields are flagged as 'incomplete' rather than throwing an error.

nodes.name: Sheets - Search for Duplicate
nodes.notes: Searches the Invoices sheet for an existing row with the same invoice number before appending. Prevents duplicate entries from re-submissions.

nodes.name: Sheets - Update Incomplete Row
nodes.notes: Updates an existing row that was previously marked incomplete. Only runs when a previous extraction was partial and this submission fills in the missing fields.

nodes.name: Slack - Notify Finance
nodes.notes: Posts to the finance channel. Uses emoji badges: ✅ complete, 🟡 incomplete extraction, ⚠️ duplicate. Lists any missing fields so the team knows to review manually.

parameters.jsCode:
const slackResp = $input.first().json;
const data = $('Merge Sheets Result').first().json;

return [{
  json: {
    success: true,
    message: data.invoiceStatus === 'duplicate'
      ? `Invoice ${data.invoiceNo} already exists — skipped write.`
      : `Invoice ${data.invoiceNo} from ${data.vendor} recorded successfully.`,
    invoiceNo: data.invoiceNo,
    vendor: data.vendor,
    amount: data.amount,
    currency: data.currency,
    dueDate: data.dueDate,
    issueDate: data.issueDate,
    invoiceStatus: data.invoiceStatus,
    extractionStatus: data.extractionStatus,
    missingFields: data.missingFields,
    fileUrl: data.cdnUrl,
    sheetsRowId: data.sheetsRowId,
    sheetsWritten: data.sheetsWritten,
    uploadedBy: data.uploadedBy,
    department: data.department,
    receivedAt: data.receivedAt,
    processedAt: new Date().toISOString()
  }
}];
